description_long: This object is a straight section of railway track, featuring two parallel steel rails held in place by a series of evenly spaced cross-ties, also known as sleepers. The rails are metallic and have a dark, slightly weathered appearance, typical of steel used in urban rail infrastructure. The cross-ties appear to be made of wood, with a rectangular profile and a slightly rough texture, running perpendicular to the rails. There is no visible ballast bed or pavement embedding, and the track is shown isolated without any surrounding ground or signage. The structure is linear and symmetrical, designed to support and guide train wheels along its length. The overall look is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and function for urban or industrial railway applications.
description: A straight section of railway track with two parallel steel rails and wooden cross-ties, isolated and without ballast or signage. The structure is robust and utilitarian, suitable for urban rail systems.
description_view_0: The railway track is viewed from one end, showing the parallel alignment of the rails and the cross-ties extending outward.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length of the track, highlighting the regular spacing of the wooden cross-ties and the straightness of the rails.
description_view_2: Viewed from the opposite end, the parallel rails and cross-ties are again visible, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, displaying the track's length and the consistent arrangement of the cross-ties.
